title: EXAMPLE 3.10 Investigating DT System Properties
summary: This example analyzes the discrete-time system y[n+1] = x[n+1]x[n], rewritten as y[n] = x[n]x[n−1], to determine several standard system properties. The page shows that the system is nonlinear, time-invariant, causal, not invertible, and BIBO-stable, with supporting algebra and counterexamples.
